acquisition.com reads when they when they get their job so I feel like it was kind of telling to set the day this way all right if you execute everything in this handbook you become an expert at selling and so for here will be for this workshop easily the top 1% or even the top 0.1% but you cannot get there without first understanding what selling is and rather than only to find it I'll set the frame with a powerful story an arrogant student interrupts a psychology seminar in scoffs look man everyone knows you can lead a horse to water you can't make a drink acting as if he just shared some profound wisdom the psychology professor paused and looked at the student that he said slowly is the best way to make yourself powerless the room fell silent you assume the environment doesn't matter you assume we can change nothing that affects whether the horse drinks but you're wrong so he made his way over to the blackboard chalk in hand if you want the horse to drink see if I can act this out in real time if you want the horse to drink you could sell to speed the night before you could remove access to water you could fix its head on so it can't look away from that water you could even start bleeding it out and the students got visibly uncomfortable there we go the professor turned back to the class his eyes sharp I could go on but I think you get the point with every change the chance the horse drinks gets closer to 100% and it's not because you asked it nicely reasoned with it or because it had a horse conversation with itself it drinks because and only because the situation makes drinking the most likely response the student puffed up but that that's manipulation it's horrible the professor squinted making a horse drink is a scientific question your belief that something is impossible because you don't like it is not science does not care about your approval he returned to his seat you can hide from the truth with any fantasy you'd like but real outcomes happen in the real world for that reason they are subject to the conditions of the real world and that can only mean one thing if you control the conditions you control the outcome and I like to set the frame for the day with this in mind because there's a lot of times that we we want to cast our power about our ability to persuade on to other people or onto situations or whatever but the reality is that if and I use this example a lot but wherever you guys are right now if I wanted to get every single person naked I genuinely believe I could do it now how could I do that well if I could control all the conditions which would be that I lock you in your in your room right I blast the heat to 200 degrees within a few hours all of you would take all of your clothes off and so it wasn't because I you had a horse conversation with yourself it's not because I persuaded you in some very compelling way it was because I controlled the conditions and so the idea here is that if we want to desire behavior or we want to desire an outcome from a prospect then we want to control as many conditions as possible and to be clear we can't control all the conditions but we want to control as many as we possibly can that maximize the likelihood of a sale and the reason I see selling as such a maybe I'm romantic about it is that it's just this it's kind of this wild expertise and trying to control chaos there's unlimited variables that affect people and we couldn't actually guarantee 100 of people buy because we can't control someone's history of reinforcement coming into a conversation now we could guarantee someone bought if we got to control their lives from the time they were born until the time they got to to our sales table we can't do that and so we just try and control as many of the variables as we possibly can and so our goal here right why do we do this is to maximize the likelihood that a qualified leads buy that's it so that is the outcome is that maximize the likelihood qualified leads become customers and so the idea here is that and because sales is additive in nature rather than zero to one sales is in that we're going to control a variable and it's going to add five percent one out of 20 people who work in a buy now buy because of this one variable we're going to add another variable that gets another one out of 20 people to buy so that gives us another five percent and I would encourage you to think about sales in this way it's controlling many variables rather than saying there's one silver bullet in in some sometimes like amazing hook can be a silver bullet sometimes an amazing message or kind of a brand these are kind of big silver bullets that take mini golden beams to accomplish but then once you have established them can create huge changes in a business you know seemingly overnight sales is not that way at least in my experience it's so much more additive that there's many small things that when put together create the outcome and uh Tyler I know that you're staying naked so good to know uh so a few strategic principles to set the day all right so one of them is that many of you if this is especially important for those of you who sell more premium stuff and the reason I find this so important is that I read this book a very long time ago I think it was called triple x sales but basically they'd interviewed a brothel manager um brothels are they were legal wherever this lady did it I think um and this is not a moral you know commentary on whether brothels are good or bad so calm down all right but I'm just saying that they were very successful in charging significantly higher prices for their services uh compared to their competition and so when talking to the brothel manager the thing that that stood out and I'm a big believer and I can learn from anybody all right so I'd encourage you to do that without having moral judgment but one of the things that they brought up was everything has to be congruent and so it's almost you're painting this picture for prospect from the time they see the ad all the way the time they give you money and if you create this frame of this is what you're buying into this is what our business is about and then at any point in that process something is off something triggers a red flag we're like well that doesn't make sense with the thing before you lose them and so I would say that above all else we want to make sure congruence is is through is true throughout the funnel so if you would like to have a very like if you guys want to have something that will kick you in the stomach and make you feel terrible this is what I would encourage you to do so this is action item number one of the day go to a brothel I'm kidding action item number one of the day is go through your entire sales process all right go through the entire sales process go through it as a as a prospect would so change the name change the email change the number whatever and actually walk through it with your team and if you don't have a team and it's just you walk through at least the click flow as a consumer might and what you might find surprising and terrible is that there's a lot of pieces that feel really clunky that don't feel smooth that don't make sense this part says it's going to do this and then we go to somewhere else because you've iterated this thing a couple times over the years and you've never actually smoothed out the edges I can almost guarantee you that before I do anything else we talk about today making sure that what you promise from the very beginning to what they actually get on a sales conversation in a call or on a zoom or whatever it is that you sell as long as that entire process is congruent you will get significantly higher lift and then once you know it's congruent then we can see what else can what can we make it congruent about and then we can start elevating things but if everything's disjointed you will not close nearly as many second buyers have very high motivation to buy for very short periods of time and motivation will be will be directly correlated with the level of deprivation they experience around the solution aka the pain they experience right now and the thing is if you ever had pain around something it's typically very short-lived like you're in a bad mood for much shorter periods of time than you think you are even when you're super sad very short period then you go about your life and something reminds you then you're sad for a short period and then you're and then you're you know you're back to normal again right and so the thing is is that it's in those moments where the motivation is sky high and we have to be there ready to make an offer right and so many of you like you could triple your businesses by simply talking to your leads within a minute and I'm going to get to a lot of tactics around this today but like I can't emphasize this enough many of you guys who would just double or triple your business if you just called your leads faster that's it you made it easier and faster for them to buy the next piece is around concision and we're going to get to all the sales tactic and stuff these are just big pillars that I thought were worth bringing up from a from a behavior perspective I I think what separates exceptional copy from from from mediocre copy is clarity the more I've been selling the more I've been marketing the more I've been persuading I have I have lost so much of my mythology around all the NLP and all these language patterns and all this stuff like most people are confused and they're distracted and they have no idea what you're talking about so like you just have to speak to them in a third grade language not because they're dumb but because they don't care they need to make it so easy for them to consume that they don't even use any mental brainpower to process what you're saying so that you can just allow the message to do the work and not let their brain try to process it and then make a judgment on it and so whenever you're in doubt about your messaging in your entire sales process or the scripting itself just look back and say can I dumb this down to a third grade reading level so that I use no words that someone has to think about in order to make a decision like across the board and I've done this more times than I can count if I want to improve a campaign I literally take all the copy all the way through the funnel and the script I put it through a reading calculator and I get it below fifth grade third grade is amazing fifth grade is usually more normal all right but you want to get it as low as grade possible and if you just do that you will see 10 20 30 50 percent improvements overnight without actually changing what you say but only how you said it because you made it easy now I've talked about this at length at our workshops that we hold here but I will for those of you who have not been here in person I want to outline a concept for you that I think is really powerful so who here has heard the concept that there are emotional buyers and there are logical buyers let me hear in the chat who here has heard that concept right great and I heard this concept as well and I believed it because somebody on a stage said it and I nodded my head and said ah that person makes more money than me therefore I will listen everything they say right after thinking about a lot more I actually don't think that's true and I'll explain why so I believe that there are high information buyers and there are low information buyers but to be clear it's not binary it's not higher low information it's not it's not logical emotional it's high too low meaning that this exists on a continuum now if we were to represent this in terms of what it looks like from a marketing perspective or the marketplace at large would look like this which is there are way more people that require more information order to make a decision and then some people require less information to make a decision now here's the cool part there's two vectors to this one is that some people naturally are more inclined here and some people are naturally born here but you can shift the whole curve this way by simply making clear messaging that educates the marketplace that makes it more likely that they'll buy now the reason that many of you and some of you guys have probably heard this stat many of you guys have probably heard that you know three percent of people in a marketplace are ready to buy 97 percent are not ready to buy yet some of you guys have may have heard this stat before okay I strongly believe it and the reason that big brands are so big and make so much more money than the than the classic kind of straight direct response style like you have no brand you have no reputation you just have arbitrage on media meaning you spend x you make y you make the spread and that's your whole business and there's nothing wrong with that it's just that you're not going to make nearly as much money as you could to be precise if you're you're all competing if that's how you do business you're all there's this this big concept that like you know people are born sales people and I hardcore disagree with that I think some people over the years leading into their job interview with you will have reproduce sales on a consistent basis the thing that maintains their behavior at a high level is culture and I'm going to dive into this really deep later but many of you you have some of the right very fast here um as a reminder if no one is buying if zero people are buying we want to make sure that we're matching the thing they want to the time specifically they want to buy it all right so sometimes this is especially true with some of you guys who are like i can't get people to buy my upsells so i'm a little out of order here but i can't get people to buy my upsells you either have the wrong thing the wrong time the wrong way or all three so what do i mean by that so right now i can tell you there's a bunch of you guys that can't close your upsells because you're doing it the wrong time so let's imagine this is a client journey this is day zero you guys are offering them to buy again at the end this is the worst time to offer it because you will miss out on two-thirds of your sales if you wait till the end typically you will want to have a day one kind of immediate upsell you'll have a 24 hour if something makes sense then you'll have a milestone if this is how you do it if you want to if you want to maximize you've got your halfway and then you've got your last chance these are all the times you actually need to sell notice that four out of five times are in the first half of the journey so right now if you're like man i can only get one out of three people to buy on the back end that makes sense that's about industry average for a last chance offer if you want to get to 70 plus percent you got to offer it sooner because think about this like uh like even like video retention if i if i had a video and people were watching my video right if i made my ads here in the last minute i only have 10 of people even watching the video at that point so even if i closed 100 of people i could close 100 of 10 right you're missing out on the boat so you want to basically make the offers when you have 10 times the audience up here even if you have a lower conversion you're still going to close more sales people have a lot of discomfort around this and the main point is that you typically for some reason you have some odd like mental belief around this because somebody like didn't hug you enough as a child when you were getting sold but but big picture when we're selling this way you just have to make sure that you're offering them more of that thing a faster version of that thing an easier version of that thing a more risk-free way of that thing to get them where they want to go fundamentally that's it and if you make a better offer you'll convert more people and if you do it the right time the right way all right now the the the money model's book talks about all the upsell stuff those upsell mechanisms i strongly encourage you to use in any kind of upsell process that is kind of what gives you the the hand to hand to make those this process is really smooth like the rollover upsell or the anchor upsell those are all classic ways of doing it or the classic upsell cool now um this next piece around lead magnet and it feeds pretty naturally into avatar i talked about better offer and lead magnet in uh the the lead implementation workshop but because offers affect literally everything in a business it still felt worthwhile to at get these people to buy but by doing that you're just trying to cater the people who are going to cause you the most headaches the only way to serve this audience is something that is entirely automated it's the only way like there are absolutely ways to make billions of dollars from this audience because there's a lot of people here but you got to find a way so that the incremental cost of serving a customer is zero that's how it has to work otherwise you got to go here it's just the nature of the business you want to make as much money as you possibly can so you can build out the person they have a certain type of demographic they're like I love these people they're awesome I can provide a ton of value to them great stop selling people that are not them and then price encourage you to make a document called why people buy whatever your thing is now let me show you the results of this if you can see it on the screen when we change the messaging and I'm like I'm not experience you are there's always more there's always a deeper way and sometimes like you get lost because you haven't made copying a while and you come back in and and you just need a refresher so the greatest thing fed out the phrases they used take those phrases put them in the ads put them in a new vsl and then we included them in the sales script guess what happened our contact rate more than doubled our call booking rate almost doubled the revenue impact just from this one just from inbound specifically almost doubled all across the board and our quality of the people who are coming in for our workshops because obviously we're constrained in terms of our square footage went up literally improved by 20 or 30 percent in terms of the quality of the people that were coming in and so the reason I bring this up is that like if you could have a double simply by changing the words you use to be more accurate because like and I'll tell you what I'll tell you what the big shift was for me I talked a lot about what I want right what I wanted was these things but what someone more deeply wants is to get out of pain and so I was talking more about promise and less about pain and so we just flipped it to be more about pain and less about promise and guess what's more motivating pain a version also side note guess what's more compliant pain a version you say let me tell you all the bad things about your life I can help you not have that versus let me promise how much you're going to make on the upside and so when I'm in like great mood marketer I'm thinking upside I'm thinking scale I'm thinking big dream big possibilities we're going to change the world but why does someone buy because they haven't taken a fucking vacation in a year because they can't be with their kids and their slack notification keeps coming up because they have this deep fear that they're never going to be able to get this business to make money without them killing themselves and when I say killing themselves I mean figuratively like working too hard right there they cannot imagine a world where someone else could actually run it and they could maybe not just just have a week where even when you do go on vacation you actually can check out you can actually be present because it's not even about leaving it's about leaving leaving the business behind emotionally or mentally so you have space to actually relax many of us entrepreneurs here have not taken a vacation in years you might have physically gone somewhere but you have not experienced the lack of stress for what feels like a decade or multiple decades that and solving that problem gets people to buy and so when I and the thing is is like I had to get refreshed on this because like we need to be reminded more than we need representative of the entire business but here's why this is so genius how much infrastructure do you need to handle this many customers the same amount that you need to handle this many customers how much revenue does a top 20 customer typically drive so how do you 5x a business in terms of top line and bottom line without increasing infrastructure nearly to the same degree imagine 5xing top line and keeping your infrastructure costs neural is the same that was the genius behind his model and this is what he's done over and over and over and over again to be able to create hundreds of billions to be there 18 hours a day and so the how and when this is we figured this out is that when we looked and we analyzed the sales calls we figured out that 78 percent of gym owners who made a purchasing decision with us had consumed at least two long form pieces of content long form being defined as over an hour or a book right and so if they consumed at least two not one but two long form pieces that's where all the buyers were coming from and so then when I realized that people were like oh so should I just try and find people who've already consumed two hours hell no try and get everybody so the purpose of vsl is to standardize your pitch and let your salesman handle specifics so what does that mean so for many of you guys you're like man no one can sell like me i'm you know it's the super specialized sale blah blah blah blah you're not that special there's a certain number of things that you say on a repeated basis to a wide variety of customers that you just need to take all those things down this just takes work and you put into a video that you make every single prospect watch and you add visuals and you add graphs you add analogies and demonstrations and stories and proof and you add those pieces together and then you have every prospect watch that and now all of a sudden your team can close because you did all of that stuff that except trying to teach them all got to pay them a king's ransom in order to be able to do that but the more you can machine the process that increases the likelihood someone buys the lower the skill is required in order to get someone to buy and the faster the training to complete that skill and the less you have to pay them and the higher your conversion and the more scalable your sales process this knocks out like 10 different problems now i don't know if i i think i think it comes up in a second um vsls are going to be integrated throughout your entire sales process and i'll explain where they are visually in a rescripted a big deal and so uh when you make your vsls pay special attention just like if you're making content to the first minute or two because that's where you're going to have a huge amount of something that you're drinking and 90 percent of americans are unaware of it and actually happens in existing households and so the next couple minutes and here's you know john john had his back pain are the types of people effects this is how it affects them this is where this is why it's happening and this is how we're going to help right and then f a q's now the f a q's part of this you can put struggling with a specific type of objection then you've probably made content to solve that objection and you probably have a testimony of someone who had that objection then bought and then had a great experience so we'd send both of those right this is how you can curate uh kind of a sales experience that maximizes likely that they buy all right like so for our team our team will prep our notes the night before or morning of so it's up to you guys um but this is kind of what i see is the boring work that a sales culture of you know will either praise and reinforce or becomes yet person and they do that across every sale they will sell more people and so that and i don't want to talk about it super long because it's it's a very obvious thing that nobody does it's not a magic we end up making more money in almost every scenario where we just say the price because we're there's we're so much more sales efficient because we don't have all these calls with people that like would in no way buy because some people like if i you know like whatever your number is if they're like no way um then they how much is the sales all convince them i just tend to be more efficient with my sales team and rather than just like crank um but if like no one's booking then maybe remove it so there's no right answer there i would say that me historically i would say the majority of maximize the likelihood that a prospect shows all right so these are 99% golden bbs there are no silver bullets like in sales process in general there's no silver bullets but especially with follow up all right so these there are there are more gains here i commonly find like it's not often that you find like doubles and triples um in business that are just lying around from like little processes follow up is just one of those rare situations where it's true like i'm so bullish on this that i very strongly considered starting a fund only around the thesis that people don't follow up with their leads and as a result we could double or triple the revenue of a business simply by doing this just working the leads well that's it all right and so what's interesting is that i find it's significantly easier to get a 50% lift in show rates than is to get a 50% lift in close rates and the net result for the business is the same so if i can get something that's really easy and gives me 50% lift in revenue i will do that first and the beauty of this whole thing with follow up is that it's just processes here it's there's almost no skill involved that's why i love this so much like you just need to get someone to do it like there's very little it's very binary did you do it did you not do it not how well did you do it very straightforward did you call them within 60 seconds yes perception of flexibility and this was the single one of the single greatest things that you can do in terms of increasing sales in a business is increasing the convenience for which people can book there's a lot of things that can improve it and the crazy thing is all of these are additive so can you double your availability and double the business yes can you double your speed to lead and also double the business yes if you do both in when you forex your business you might that sounds crazy it also could be true so um two examples so I came in contact with the business that was a relatively large business in the weight loss space and they had astonishing row as which is not common in a very commoditized space like that and so I I tried to look at their sales process look at the marketing I was like trying to figure it and one thing jumped out at me was that the founder was going in and this is like a pretty big business and he was just a maniac but he would go in he would look at every scheduled call for the day read the answers to the applications and questions that they had selected and then he would manually color code delete calls off calendar and and code them and move them to the right sales person by doing that he was able to eliminate like two-thirds of calls in the calendar to have the very low likelihood of closing and maximizes closes throughput with higher quality leads and I saw that and I was like I think this is the reason that your business is more profitable than everyone else's and I bring it up to say if you cannot waste time on leads that aren't going to buy you will make more time for the leads that will and so I'll give you a second example which number one timeshare salesman in the United States happens to live in my building and he's the number one timeshare salesman for the number one timeshare company 3000 sales reps he's number one five out of six years big thing that I asked him that he did that was the big breakthrough he got the CEO in his tiny little market to give him all the best leads because he was the best closer and he five x the production of that store with the same lead volume because he said don't waste the bad leads on me put all the good leads with me and I'll close all of them you gave a good lead to a bad sales rep and you lost that sale and I could have made it you gave a bad lead to me I was going to lose it and so is that guy you might as well let him lose it and let me make the make the good one it's force optimization optimizing the highest likelihood purchasers with the highest likelihood person to help them buy so what do we do about this at the very beginning we talked about the avatars if we know that the people who are most likely to buy have these things in common if someone has four of the five things you can either make it binary in that they have to have all five in order to buy but then there's some things that are continuums if someone has to have at least a million dollars a year in revenue then if they have five million they're probably more likely to buy so it might be a higher rated lead so you put that lead scoring in place and after like man this takes technology it's like dude it's 2025 like ai's coming like if you can't lead score then like come on you know um like you have to you have to figure this stuff out like I can't I'm I'm I'm not going to share screen with you guys now like that is beyond the scope of this but you have to be able to score your leads and then you have to be able to route them to closers who are the best closers so if from zero to a hundred million dollars uh in in in revenue in like 24 months I think some of you guys have heard of grunts there are very big like gummy bear greens gummy bear thing the thing that startled me or that I found really interesting is that they hammer they didn't go one to one to seven they hammer the first four days so day one through four two emails a day one text first four days so you're doing 12 reach outs right four sorry eight emails four texts just within uh the first four days now they have zero sales team this is just pure e-commerce but just to give you an idea of how much they're willing to hammer because they did all the math and realized that those first four days is where they maximize the likelihood that someone's going to buy and so that should give you some idea of how hard you need to hit those first few days and so for us call within five minutes you saw the stats obviously 60 seconds is better but call within five minutes we do it we double dial immediately so first call gets blocked by almost most phone settings nowadays the second one can connect literally call it'll disconnect call again it'll go through um speed alone increases both show up rates and close rates so like this whole getting more leads to pick up thing it increases everything down the funnel so if you call them faster you you get more leads to schedule more than we'll show and you'll have higher close rates because you are closing them closer to the pain and you will know when you're calling fast enough when people respond with wow that was fast so this is a this is a typical setup day one this is super tactical but call one we call it twice and then we text and leave voicemail we repeat that two more times you're like wait a second call twice and text that's try and act like we're trying to get in touch with them does double dialing hurt pick up rate no it it helps pick rates um bio break requested soon dude it's been it's been an hour and seven minutes then act like you're trying to get a hold of them yeah this is a skill issue Tyler by the way your bio break um so day two through three uh we'll call twice and then we text out for the second call this maintains the pressure without being kind of overbearing so we'll call two times we're in the day again double dolly time and then we'll text after the second and then four through seven it's one call in one text a day all right so you notice we hit super hard day one through three all right um and by doing it with through four through seven we'll catch weekends uh and sometimes we have like we have noticed this for us is that there are some people who just always were they catch up on stuff on weekends and so making sure that you extend the follow-up through day seven we catch some of those people who are weekend responders which gets you to a total of 11 attempts across seven days which gets about 80 percent of possible sales just on research that we have so to me this is like the literally the 80 20 of outreach or rather a follow-up okay automated versus manual wonders this is this is uh uh the the housekeeping but important all right make automation obvious and make manual obvious what i would encourage you not to do is try to make your automation seem manual like make them clear if it's manual get a blue iphone give it to your sales guys and make sure they know that it's from a person if it's automated be clear like this is a scheduled message this is a 24 hour reminder just make it very clear all right so these are the three automated reminder times that we have 12 24 and three hours before so just like we use standard calendar software whatever like the the thing that comes with it automatically we want them to look automated that's how we do it all right um also you see that little prototype at the bottom there which is you want to include the area code that you're going to call from if it's over the phone or again the link if it's going to be a zoom uh because it'll just increase the picker rates next the manual text we sent from a real cell phone the night before frost at 6 p.m and morning of at 8 a.m 60 minutes prior and that's going to be relative to their time zone all right that's what we do uh we send multiple messages in a row kind of like you text a real person so it's like you text text text without a response because that's how people actually text rather than like these long blocks of paragraphs all right and uh we open with a probing question about their interest then we compliment them aca and then ask them if they want to move up the time to today so wait a second did you catch that when we manually text them and we confirm appointments we try to pull them forward because if you pull forward appointments sure it's go up now if we have no response to manual tasks text what we do is we escalate scarcity so that's where we say hey we have limited slots or we've limited availability for that day or that time uh did you check my message and then hey i'll have to remove from that spot until i hear back is this still a good time is there the two messages that we send after someone hasn't responded you know sam the the thing with the personalized selfie videos we used to do that and we ended up not seeing a difference so just it was a lot of work and that's the only reason i bring it up if it's worked for you by all means keep doing it but it was a it was a ton of work for our team uh because like we'll do whatever work is required and sending the personalized videos didn't actually we didn't get a meaningful list lift and shows so just yeah regardless that is kind of how we see automated versus manual hopefully that shall be guys out okay now bamfam book a meeting from a meeting if you every single person here should bamfam okay in the chat real quick tell me who doesn't know what bamfam means just give me a like you don't know okay so we have at least one person who doesn't know what bamfam is jonathan you don't know what it is either okay then i will explain what bamfam is okay so sharon sravats is the one who taught me this and i love it and have used it ever since in terms of the acronym so bamfam stands for book meeting from a meeting the reason this is so important is that this needs to become a way of life all right so we had uh we have a portfolio company um which is an insurance company and in that business it's very like long proposals of like very large you know uh insurance policies sometimes million dollar policies like very large policies right and one of the things that happened is we kept losing lots of prospects throughout the funnel and so i was walking through the sales process and i was like okay well what happens after we you know do the intake call they're like oh well we you know take all the research we do this you know we make this big proposal and then we email it to them and we asked them to book another time in the email and i was like oh my god really it's like yeah i was like well there's probably a triple in this business sitting here it's like what do you mean i was like bamfam dude like you get on the set call you take the information you set the close call and then instead of sending the proposal tell them you'll go over the proposal that you prepared for them on the close call and if you don't close on the close call before you get off the phone set the next close call you always book a meeting from a meeting you never leave a lead in no man's land this has to become a way of life every lead should know when they're going to talk to you again and i'm going to overcome some of these these these issues some of these beliefs that some of you guys have all right so we never end a call without booking the next appointment if they don't close you pull up your calendar right then and you lock in the next call so the whole like we'll circle you know we'll circle back offline is a guaranteed drop off between calls does not work complete waste of time you have them right there if you cannot get them to book a time when they are talking to you how do you think you're gonna get them to book a time when you are texting them or emailing them you were both there pull up both your calendars find the time right it's the fastest most efficient way to communicate all right and so being non-committal and this is a huge point you have to explain to salespeople being non-committal about the next appointment is an objection right so handle it now while the pain is at its highest at its freshest because if you can't close them literally after doing a pitch you're not going to close them later when the problem is in the rear view right and so this is the highest likelihood moment to book the next call it's also the most efficient and the least work it's very rarely if all these things line up there's no logical reason to not book the call at that moment and so the result of this is that people will stop falling through the cracks sales will increase just make more and so I'll confront so my I went I went home obviously for for my mom's thing and I had to that's all my cousin so you just started a business the expert paradox I left I mentioned this a bit earlier which is if you're the only person who can close your organization I promise you it's not like you're some unicorn and no one else can close right it's that you are actually bad at selling and good at being an expert really take that in for a second if you just demonstrate tremendous expertise people will buy but you might be terrible at selling and so for me or at least there's levels of sales level one sales as you can sell level two sales as you teach somebody else to sell level three sales you teach a team of people to sell right and so we want to get you to be a level three type sales person we'll be a sales master a master trainer not a master salesman much higher leverage right and if you're exceptional at it it's usually because you sell the wrong way you sell leaning on your authority expertise rather than leaning on a very good process but that being said the way to get out of this is the v1 version is take 20 close calls that you have transcribe your calls put it into a gpt and say or you put an acq ai and say create one script based on all the stuff using that I have here mixed with diagnostic and the closure framework boom and then it'll take all the stuff we have pop it in easy now follow your own script once you have this new this is important once you have this new script you follow that script and the can still close deals are you getting the difference here if you if you have 20 different if you if you take 20 different paths to close to be fair there's really never 20 paths there's every sale has 20 grand and shut the fuck up now I'll give you a little tip there the tip here especially if you have some newer sales guys you can include it's expensive before you introduce price and it works fine so if some guys get a little nervous it just like just say it's expensive and then you can say the price hey it's expensive and they say it almost like people feel like I don't know better about it so you can say it's expensive because it actually acts as a price anchor believe it or not because people what's nice about the word expensive is that everyone's idea of what's expensive is personal so it's like a it's a it's an amorphous anchor so if someone says it's expensive for me I'm gonna think oh my god it's a gazillion dollars and if the person's rich and you say it's expensive and then you say the number it's like having a price thank you and they're like oh my god that's not expensive I'm in and if the person is poor and you said it's expensive at least you're prepared so it works on both sides now I want to be really clear about something that I have really thought I've wrestled a lot with do I talk about tone in this I don't think I do no I don't okay so I'm gonna do a quick a quick uh you guys get with a little a little side quest on tonality you guys get with a little side quest on tonality okay so I have spent a long time analyzing speech patterns to maximize persuasive effect and I have found that there is a lot of mythology in selling around tonality and I would like to put that to rest so that you cannot waste any more time or nearly as much time as I have trying to coach tone so ready for ready for some mind blowing mind blowing stuff there are three sales constants which I consider inagriant sales tone now sales tone does not mean sounding salesy it means it's a persuasive tone if you sound salesy it's likely unpersuasive all right and there's three elements and they're very obvious one is you have to be loud enough that they can hear you two is you have to speak clearly enough so you have to enunciate your words so they can understand you number three you have to speak slowly enough they can comprehend loud clear and slow if I talk like that you will understand what I'm saying so that it maximizes the likelihood you take the next action when I ask you to take it right this never changes now what else can you do with your voice you have your tone or we can say inflection if you want so like this do you have a question mark uh and then you have pause so when you are selling you want to breathe the script meaning you have to know and memorize the script so well that you don't think about what the words you're saying are because if you know what the words are that you're going to say you will say them naturally most people sound like they're reading a script because it means they don't know the words well enough if you know the words well enough it sounds like you're just talking because you've said them a hundred times that's the difference now if you do that you will have natural changes in cadence and rhythm and provided you are loud enough speak clearly enough and speak slowly enough people think that it sounds natural and it only sounds natural because you've practiced it that's the idea now these two things are the only things that are very important for well that's important because you have to do that because if they don't understand you they can't buy what you're saying but these two are the the the money makers all right so what are these two this is when you ask a question or when I say John John I do that because what is it what happens in the human language when you do this John you solicit response that's the only thing it tells us is that when someone raises there when they raise their voice it means that they're asked they're they're soliciting a response from you also what happens when you pause you draw attention to the words that you're saying when you pause see it seems like I'm emphasizing something but I'm not I'm just doing it for emphasis right but when you create literally you say a word and pause draws attention to the word you just said right so at what point in the sale will we want to draw a ton of attention and solicit a response when you ask them to buy which is why you raise your voice and say you ready to come out and you shut the fuck up eight seconds sales people that added eight seconds of silence after they make an ask close 32 percent more sales 32 percent more sales eight seconds I want to like let's be real you ready to do this you ready to rock and roll awesome eight seconds eight seconds which functionally means you let them talk and if it takes a long time and any person who's been experienced salesperson knows sometimes you have 60 seconds of silence and you just shut up you just let them think let them process you don't want to sell your way out of a sale they haven't responded yet okay so it is an amateur thing to sell people out of a sale now when we are going through the script if we want to emphasize a certain point what would we do so give me another example notice that wasn't a question that was a command give me another example tell me tell me another specific hopefully though by understanding these five things you can get out of all the mythology around selling and so when you're actually making scripts the only thing you need to do for me quote tonality perspective is make sure they memorize it so that these things happen naturally and then you just only have to denote in the script where are they going to raise their voice and where do they pause that's it that makes it wildly simple and you can denote inflections with a question mark because that's how people normally do it and you can denote pauses the way we do it is one period or multiple periods short pause long pause that's it so when you're writing your scripts you might have a question mark after a word because it shows that it's going up and then you might have dots or a single dot just like you'd normally pause it will make your life so much easier if you write out your scripts like that i told you that was a little bit of a side quest but we completed it fantastic all right now where is my where is my super suit okay here we go now objection handling this was the third i think most requested topic second or third most requested topic yes third most requested topic all right so i'm gonna i have good news i got bad news for objection handling the good news is i'm going to really dramatically change many of y'all's lives because i have thought about this so much um and the bad news is if you're obsessing about objection handling you've already lost so i will give you the most consolidated way to solve this for yourself but also understand that like you're only going to get 10 or 20 percent of your sales from people who are not buying after you ask the first time and that's because if you've done your job correctly and everything else in the funnel you have a really qualified prospect that's seen you know a handful of high quality content from you they've already watched another hour of stuff of a you know some sort of vsl or some sort of pre-frame before you talking to them if you're not cl- like you're it's it's unlikely that you're going to save them from the dead right now i'm going to give you the hand to hands around this stuff and we'll cover it but don't think that if you're if you want to get your team from like a 20 percent close rate to a 40 percent close rate it's not going to be from objection handling you might get them from 2025 but to go from like 20 to 40 it's going to be in the disco it's going to be in the setup it's going to be the offer and it's going to be from cutting words and sentences out of the script okay now people will obsess here like i said but you only close 10 or 20 percent of your deals here so if you have these a lot you have to go back earlier and practice the process but if you are going to be here let me tell you i've changed my mind about handling objections so the objective of sales in general after you've established there's a clear reason for them to buy the thing is to ask as many times for the sale as you possibly can so think about that think about that because it's going to make people uncomfortable the goal on the sales call is to ask as many times as you can to get them to buy because the person you ask the most time has the highest likelihood of getting them to buy caveat problem people don't like being asked to buy a lot and so how do you ask as many times as you can without getting them to want to stop having the conversation with you therefore preventing you from asking again well you have to ask in a way that does not offend them so we prat yeah there we go it's almost like i wrote this thing okay so most of this is matching your response to their objection and doing so in a good tone right so if you speak clearly speak loudly speak slowly they're not going to sense that you're being aggressive right that you're being combative and so asking questions then to also do that now this is the this is the very large change that i've had personally in my thinking process around objection overcomes so i used to drill my team on every type of objection talk there's five objections you've got time you've got spouse you've got money all this other stuff right you've got these different these different things um i have since come to this this conclusion that is tentative um that you can really just memorize six or seven of them um well there's six or seven that are universal memorize three or four of them and if you can just say those ones and just know them like the back of your hand they work with everything and that's why they're so powerful because i have less and less belief that you overcoming someone's concern is the reason they buy unless it's a logistical concern which i don't really consider a true objection someone's like i can't make that time that's different then like i can't afford it i'm not sure blah blah okay so these are the seven universal closes if someone says no matter what you're doing if they don't want to buy or they just say anything that's not like you say hey you ready to get started ready to rock and roll whatever then they don't say yes you say totally hear you do you mind if i ask you a question real quick do you think this thing is something that can help you get close to your goal that's it really straightforward if they say yes then you're like awesome you can be like awesome let's rock and roll and sometimes you just close them right there now sometimes they don't say that and you can sense that they're a little bit more weird and you're like okay well then what's your main concern that's loop one no matter what they say totally like they don't say yes do you mind if i ask you a question do you think this is gonna be close to your goal great so what's your main concern all right then they tell you their concern then you say that might be the perfect reason you should do it doesn't matter what they say i don't have the time i think not having time I'd be the perfect reason you should do it i have to talk to my spouse i think the fact that you have to talk just might be the perfect reason you should do it like the fact that you have to include your partner for a financial decision like this might be the reason that your business should do something like this i think the fact that you don't have time to do this is why you should do it i think the fact you don't have money to do this is why you should do it it. I'm not really sure. Okay. Totally understand. Hypothetically, if this were a 10 out of 10, if this were perfect, if this were amazing unicorn, would you do it? Yes? Okay. What's different about what you have in your mind there versus what we're helping you with here? They say yes or no. If they say, and then you're like, great, then let's rock and roll. If they still don't want to do it, and you're like, okay, let's zoom all the way out. I've got Jacob behind me smiling because he's been through all these 100 times. Each of these, the zoom out is, hey, let's be clear here. You're trying to lose weight. We saw weight loss. What's getting lost in the mix here? They get close in the weeds. So, to give you guys perspective on this, this is the first week that we run every single sales person through. All right. And so, why don't you walk through what their kind of first week looks like? Yeah. So I mean, a lot of the first week when we onboard a sales rep is mainly around getting the script in their hands and getting as many reps as possible. And so when we look at like this calendar, the first thing that we do, and we'll dive into like talking about how we create culture and how we curate the culture. But the more I think about like getting a rep in, it's a lot about setting the proper expectation. And so a lot of their first day is all going through our handbook. And really the handbook is, I mean, it's beautiful. It's a guide on how to win at acquisition.com. It's the best. And be like a very, very good sales rep here. And so they're going through the handbook, they're reading through that. And a lot of this does two things. One, it shows them exactly what to do. And two, it's just like an objection with a sale. I like to see this as pre-handling objections for the sales rep and getting the right behaviors and beliefs like ingrained in them from day one. And so as they go through that process, they're like, oh, this is what it's like to be at acquisition.com. Now, when they go through that, the whole first week, as you can see here, is like all around. They're shadowing, they're bathing in good sales calls. So we have the best sales, just bathing. They're soaking in them. And they're listening to them. They're shadowing the best reps. So I love having them here in person. Even if we have remote reps, we'll fly them out. And they'll be in person. They'll listen to the top people that be like, Hey, what'd you do there? Why'd you do that? How'd this work? And so they're going through and just understanding and seeing and soaking in bathing the calls. From there, we want to get them into, yes, they do 12-hour days. So we've tried to get them to do a little bit less because there was a point where we were like, hey, they want to do 12-hour days. And a lot of that comes to how we set up the culture and the expectations we set. And I'll touch on that for a second because it's so important. The manual shows sales reps how to win. But then when they come into the environment, when they're shadowing, when they're watching the calls, everyone's doing what the manual says. And so it makes it really real. Like it, a lot of times you can say to do something, but if you don't actually do it, no one believes you. And so when sales reps come into our culture, they read this stuff and they're like, wow, this is really dialed. This is really good. And then they look at the top performing reps and really every rep on our team and they're like, wait, they're actually doing all these things. And so it creates this like environment where everyone just wants to push. They want to open up their calendar. They want to continue to work longer hours because that's what everyone does. Because the top rep here works seven and he's working seven, 12 and we're like, dude, he's like, well, my fiance is coming in like six months. So I'm just like, I got nothing to do but sell. And it's just like, it's again, a big part of this though is like, because Jacob's, Jacob, you know, built this team. This is the first thing that I think he built from scratch. And he got to have the experience of having, you know, a couple of early reps that were not culture fits. And he got to see what it was like when you remove the cancers and the thing that think this is like my telling you guys, the cancers, the obvious cancers are obvious and you should have already removed them. But the cancers that are less obvious are they're decent. It's the five out of 10 sales guys that just don't try to win. Like I'll take a dude who's hustling and a three out of 10 over a six out of 10 guy who just like clocks in and clocks out every day of the week, because I know that that guy who's a three out of 10 will be an eight in 30 days. We just like, we just know because fundamentally, if you put the process as king, everyone is secondary to the process. No one is above the process. Like we run a new England's Patriots type of sales team. No one is above the script. No one is above the process because this script in this process came from the aggregate lives of many more sales people than you could live in a lifetime. So it's like, this is the cumulation of all of our learnings. Young Padawan do not believe that you can outlive a hundred lifetimes of sales. And so that's the perspective that we take with these guys. And at least, I mean, obviously we come in a privileged situation where we're rather earned, but the people who come in at acquisition.com 100% of our sales reps were number one at their last job. We just don't accept anyone who was a number one and can't prove they were a number one at their last role. And not one person who has come in who was a number one at their last role becomes number one here within at least three months. I think the fastest is like three-ish months that we've had someone go from like, I came in as, and they're all savages.
